source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane/Lifting Device - Fire

event.top_event: Fire from overpressurization and rupture of fuel gas filter vessel
actions_taken: One 150 lb wheeled fire extinguisher deployed
Four 30 lb hand-held fire extinguishers deployed
Fire fought for three minutes until extinguished
Area monitored for thirty minutes post-extinguishment
event.summary: On 9 May 2015 at approximately 1728 hours, the MAK-F960A Fuel Gas Filter vessel experienced catastrophic overpressurization during facility restart following a pressure upset. The vessel top was blown off, struck a nearby handrail creating sparks that ignited streaming fuel gas. The incident resulted in a fire that was extinguished by operators using portable fire extinguishers. Root causes included severe corrosion of vessel mounting tabs, failed pressure safety valves both upstream and downstream of the filter, unauthorized modifications to the filter vessels that removed differential gauges and altered level indicators, and a single-stage pressure reduction system that was inadequate for the operating conditions.
recommendations: Lake Jackson District reported no recommendations to regional office
key_phrases: Pressure Safety High upset on Low Pressure Separator
Severe corrosion of T-bolt anchor tabs
False fluid readings from modified level indicators
Dished orifice plates indicating chronic overpressurization

threats.name: Overpressurization of fuel gas filter vessel
Failure of pressure safety valves to relieve at set pressure

consequences.name: Property damage to electrical systems and equipment

controls.name: Pressure Safety Valve on MBF-V940 Fuel Gas Scrubber
controls.barrier_role: Prevent overpressurization upstream of fuel gas filter
controls.lod_basis: Primary pressure relief device upstream of MAK-F960A

supporting_text: The Pressure Safety Valve (PSV) on the MBF-V940 Fuel Gas Scrubber was set for 270 PSI, it did not relieve until 390 PSI.
During testing of safety devices the PSV on MBF-V940 was set to relieve at 270 PSI; it did not relieve until 390 PSI, thus allowing 390 PSI or greater pressure to the MAK-F960A Fuel Gas Filter.

controls.name: Pressure Safety Valve on MAK-F960B Fuel Gas Filter
controls.barrier_role: Prevent overpressurization of fuel gas filter vessel
controls.lod_basis: Secondary pressure relief device on parallel filter vessel

supporting_text: The Pressure Safety Valve (PSV) on the MAK-F960B Fuel Gas Filter was set for 270 PSI, it did not relieve until 290 PSI.
The PSV on the MAK-F960B was set to relieve at 270 PSI; it did not relieve until 290 PSI.

controls.name: Vessel structural integrity and corrosion management
controls.barrier_role: Maintain vessel pressure rating through corrosion prevention
controls.lod_basis: Structural design and maintenance to prevent vessel failure

supporting_text: The MAK-F960A was found severely corroded on the tabs that anchor the T-bolts for the top to be secured to the body of vessel. The lack of mitigation of the corrosion, derated the vessels pressure capability.
The corroded tabs weakened the vessels capability of handling any over pressurization above the Maximum Working Pressure.

controls.name: Differential pressure gauges on fuel gas filters
controls.barrier_role: Provide operator indication of filter element condition and need for replacement
controls.lod_basis: Instrumentation to detect filter clogging and prevent overpressurization

supporting_text: Lessee had modified both MAK-F960 A and B Fuel Gas Filter vessels from original design, Lessee removed differential gauges from the system, therefore Operators had no indication of when filters needed to be replaced.
The Lessee had removed the differential gauges from system, therefore Operators had no indication of when filter elements needed to be changed.

controls.name: Level Safety High (LSH) and Sight Glasses on fuel gas filters
controls.barrier_role: Provide accurate indication of fluid level in filter vessels
controls.lod_basis: Instrumentation to detect filter saturation and prevent clogging

supporting_text: The change of location of Level Safety High (LSH) and Sight Glasses gave the Operators a false fluid reading, causing the filters to become soaked with fluids and clogged.
The location of the Level Safety High (LSH) and Sight Glasses were modified at the same time; thus giving the Operators false fluid readings.

controls.name: Single-stage pressure reduction system
controls.barrier_role: Reduce inlet pressure from 945-1290 PSI to safe operating pressure
controls.lod_basis: Primary pressure control for fuel gas system

supporting_text: The fuel gas system had only one cut in the pressure, system was reducing gas pressure from a operating high of 1290 PSI to a high of 170 PSI.

Investigation revealed the gas flows from the MBF-V620 Gas Scrubber and MAF-V500 Glycol Contactor at a normal operating range from 945 PSI to a high of 1290 PSI. The gas pressure is then reduced a single cut, to 170 PSI before it is sent to the MBF-V940 Fuel Gas Scrubber.

controls.name: Ignition source isolation and hot work procedures
controls.barrier_role: Prevent ignition of released fuel gas
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to isolate ignition sources during operations

supporting_text: WERE PRECAUTIONS OR ACTIONS TAKEN TO ISOLATE KNOWN SOURCES OF IGNITION PRIOR TO THE ACCIDENT? NO
The striking of the top against nearby hand rail caused sparks, igniting the streaming gas from the vessel.

controls.name: Emergency response and fire suppression equipment
controls.lod_basis: Portable fire extinguishers and emergency response procedures

supporting_text: Operators manned one (1) 150 lb. wheeled fire extinguisher and four (4) 30 lb. hand held fire extinguishers. Operators fought fire for three (3) minutes until extinguished, then monitored area of fire for additional thirty (30) minutes.
PIC selected Operators to fight fire, directed everyone else to stay ready in muster area.

controls.name: Management of Change (MOC) process for equipment modifications
controls.barrier_role: Ensure modifications do not compromise safety systems
controls.lod_basis: Procedural control to evaluate impact of design changes

supporting_text: Lessee had modified both MAK-F960 A and B Fuel Gas Filter vessels from orginal design, Lessee removed differental gauges from the system.
All modifications were completed according to Operators in August 2014.

controls.name: Preventive maintenance and inspection program for pressure vessels
controls.barrier_role: Detect and mitigate corrosion and equipment degradation
controls.lod_basis: Scheduled inspection and maintenance to maintain vessel integrity

supporting_text: The MAK-F960A was found severely corroded on the tabs that anchor the T-bolts for the top to be secured to the body of vessel. The lack of mitigation of the corrosion, derated the vessels pressure capability.
Lessee did not have any record of the filters being changed on the two filter units, as well operators stated they had never changed filters.

controls.name: Pressure Safety Valve testing and calibration program
controls.barrier_role: Ensure PSVs operate at design set pressure
controls.lod_basis: Scheduled testing and maintenance of pressure relief devices

supporting_text: During testing of safety devices the PSV on MBF-V940 was set to relieve at 270 PSI; it did not relieve until 390 PSI.
Nearly every month in the year prior to the incident, the Daniel Senior Meter orifice plates were found to be dished (deformed) upon inspection by measurement personnel.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
